# exportarr

![Version: 4.0.0](https://img.shields.io/badge/Version-4.0.0-informational?style=flat-square) ![Type: application](https://img.shields.io/badge/Type-application-informational?style=flat-square) ![AppVersion: v2.3.0](https://img.shields.io/badge/AppVersion-v2.3.0-informational?style=flat-square)
A Helm Chart to export Prometheus metrics for Arr applications.

## Requirements

Kubernetes: `>=1.19.0-0`

| Repository | Name | Version |
|------------|------|---------|
| oci://registry-1.docker.io/homeylabcharts | qbittorrent-exporter | 1.1.0 |
| oci://registry-1.docker.io/homeylabcharts | tdarr-exporter | 2.1.0 |

A Helm Chart to deploy [exportarr](https://github.com/onedr0p/exportarr), an app that gathers Prometheus metrics from `Arr` instances. See the upstream repository for the full list of supported applications.

This chart can optionally install a Prometheus exporter for the following, as subchart dependencies:
1. `qbittorrent` using the [qbittorrent-exporter](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/qbittorrent-exporter) chart.
2. `Tdarr` using the [tdarr-exporter](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/tdarr-exporter) chart.

This allows you to consolidate different `Arr`-related exporters into a single helm chart. See the [Additional Exporters](#additional-exporters) section for more information.

## Add Chart Repo
```bash
helm repo add homeylab https://homeylab.github.io/helm-charts/
# update the chart, this can also be run to pull new versions of the chart for upgrades
helm repo update homeylab
```

## Prerequisites
Each `Arr` instance you enable under `exportarr.apps.<app>` needs a reachable `url` and, unless the target app has auth disabled, either an inline `apiKey` or an `existingSecret` (the secret's `secretKey` must hold the API key). Multiple instances of the same app (e.g. two `radarr` instances) are supported since `apps.<app>` is a list — give each a unique `name`.

If you enable the optional `qbittorrent-exporter` or `tdarr-exporter` subcharts, see their prerequisites in their own charts ([qbittorrent-exporter](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/qbittorrent-exporter), [tdarr-exporter](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/tdarr-exporter)) — the [Additional Exporters](#additional-exporters) section below covers passthrough values for both.

## Install
```bash
helm install exportarr homeylab/exportarr -n exportarr --create-namespace

# with own values file - recommended
helm install -f my-values.yaml exportarr homeylab/exportarr -n exportarr --create-namespace
```

### OCI Registry Support
```bash
helm install exportarr -n exportarr oci://registry-1.docker.io/homeylabcharts/exportarr --version X.Y.Z --create-namespace

# with own values file - recommended
helm install -f my-values.yaml exportarr -n exportarr oci://registry-1.docker.io/homeylabcharts/exportarr --version X.Y.Z --create-namespace
```

### Install Example
Only some options are shown, view `values.yaml` for an exhaustive list.

<details closed>
<summary>custom-values.yaml</summary>
<br>

```yaml
# custom-values.yaml
exportarr:
  metrics:
    serviceMonitor:
      enabled: true
      additionalLabels:
        app: exportarr
    # optional: render a single umbrella-wide PrometheusRule (requires the Prometheus Operator CRD)
    prometheusRule:
      enabled: true
      rules:
        - alert: ExportarrTargetDown
          annotations:
            summary: exportarr metrics endpoint is down.
            description: Prometheus cannot scrape exportarr ({{ $labels.instance }}).
          expr: up{job=~".*exportarr.*"} == 0
          for: 5m
          labels:
            severity: warning
  apps:
    radarr:
    - name: main
      enabled: true
      url: "https://radarr.somedomain/"
      apiKey: "someApiKey" # provide here or `existingSecret` section
    sonarr:
    - name: main
      enabled: true
      url: "https://sonarr.somedomain/"
      apiKey: "someApiKey"
      extraEnv:
        ENABLE_ADDITIONAL_METRICS: true # example specifying extraEnv
    prowlarr:
    - name: main
      enabled: true
      url: "https://prowlarr.somedomain/"
      apiKey: "someApiKey"
    bazarr:
    - name: main
      enabled: true
      url: "https://bazarr.somedomain/"
      apiKey: "someApiKey"

## additional exporters ##
qbittorrent-exporter:
  enabled: false

tdarr-exporter:
  enabled: false
```
</details>
<br>

## Upgrade
```bash
helm upgrade exportarr homeylab/exportarr -n exportarr

# with own values file - recommended
helm upgrade -f my-values.yaml exportarr homeylab/exportarr -n exportarr
```

### OCI Registry Support
```bash
helm upgrade exportarr -n exportarr oci://registry-1.docker.io/homeylabcharts/exportarr --version X.Y.Z

# with own values file - recommended
helm upgrade -f my-values.yaml exportarr -n exportarr oci://registry-1.docker.io/homeylabcharts/exportarr --version X.Y.Z
```

### Potential Breaking Updates
_The table below lists chart version upgrades that may introduce breaking changes or require manual steps._

| Start Chart Version | Target Chart Version | Upgrade Steps |
| ------------------- | -------------------- | ------------- |
| `3.1.X` | `4.0.0` | BREAKING: `exportarr.image` schema renamed (`repository` -> `registry`, `name` -> `repository`) and the `helm test` values moved from `exportarr.testImage.*` to `exportarr.tests.image.*` (and `testImage.path` -> `tests.path`). Also implements the previously-inert PrometheusRule and adds `exportarr.tests.enabled` + `exportarr.podLabels`; optional subcharts bumped (qbittorrent-exporter `1.0.1` -> `1.1.0`, tdarr-exporter `2.0.1` -> `2.1.0`). **Read the [Migrating to 4.0.0](#migrating-to-400) section before upgrading.** |
| `3.0.X` | `3.1.0` | Optional `tdarr-exporter` subchart bumped `1.2.0` -> `2.0.0` (hardened `securityContext`, standardized image schema, `appVersion` -> upstream `v3.0.0`); passthrough value keys are unchanged. **Only affects installs with `tdarr-exporter.enabled`** — see the subchart's [Migrating to 2.0.0](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/blob/main/charts/tdarr-exporter/README.md#migrating-to-200) notes. |
| `2.1.X` | `3.0.0` | Backing `qbittorrent-exporter` subchart swapped image/schema, hardened `securityContext` on all exportarr instances, inline `apiKey` now renders into a chart-managed Secret. **Read the [Migrating to 3.0.0](#migrating-to-300) section in full before upgrading.** |
| `1.X.X` | `2.0.0` | `exportarr.apps.<app>` (radarr/sonarr/etc.) changed from an object to a list, allowing multiple instances of the same `Arr` app (e.g. `radarr1`, `radarr2`). `exportarr.testCurlImage` was renamed to `exportarr.testImage`. |

### Migrating to 4.0.0
This release standardizes the image schema and the `helm test` values to match the other charts in this repo, and implements the previously-inert `prometheusRule` block. Only the value renames are breaking — if you never overrode the image or test-image values, the defaults are unchanged and no action is needed.

- **`exportarr.image` schema renamed (breaking).** The old block stored the registry in `repository` and the image path in `name`; both are renamed to the standard scheme. Move any overrides:

  | Old | New |
  | --- | --- |
  | `exportarr.image.repository` (held the registry, e.g. `ghcr.io`) | `exportarr.image.registry` |
  | `exportarr.image.name` (held the repository, e.g. `onedr0p/exportarr`) | `exportarr.image.repository` |

- **`exportarr.testImage.*` moved to `exportarr.tests.image.*` (breaking).** Move any overrides:

  | Old | New |
  | --- | --- |
  | `exportarr.testImage.repository` | `exportarr.tests.image.registry` |
  | `exportarr.testImage.name` | `exportarr.tests.image.repository` |
  | `exportarr.testImage.tag` | `exportarr.tests.image.tag` |
  | `exportarr.testImage.pullPolicy` | `exportarr.tests.image.pullPolicy` |
  | `exportarr.testImage.path` | `exportarr.tests.path` |

  The old `testImage:` keys are no longer read. Helm does not reject unknown values, so a stale block is silently ignored rather than erroring — remove it.
- **PrometheusRule now implemented (additive).** `exportarr.metrics.prometheusRule` (`enabled`/`labels`/`rules`) previously rendered nothing; it now renders a single umbrella-wide `PrometheusRule`. Default `enabled: false` — no change unless you opt in.
- **`exportarr.tests.enabled` added (additive).** Toggles the `helm test` connection-check Pod (default `true`). `exportarr.tests.image.pullPolicy` is now honoured (it was previously ignored, so the Pod fell back to Kubernetes' default pull policy).
- **`exportarr.podLabels` added (additive).** Optional labels applied to all exportarr pods (default `{}`).
- **Optional subcharts bumped (only if enabled).** `qbittorrent-exporter` `1.0.1` -> `1.1.0` and `tdarr-exporter` `2.0.1` -> `2.1.0`, both adding their own PrometheusRule support. See each subchart's README.

### Migrating to 3.0.0
This version hardens the umbrella chart and swaps the backing image for the optional `qbittorrent-exporter` subchart. Review all items before upgrading.

- **`qbittorrent-exporter` subchart swapped (breaking, only if enabled).** The optional `qbittorrent-exporter` dependency bumped `0.1.0` -> `1.0.0`, moving from the unmaintained `caseyscarborough/qbittorrent-exporter` (Java) to the actively maintained `martabal/qbittorrent-exporter` (Go). Its values schema changed (keys renamed, new options) — see the subchart's own [Migrating to 1.0.0](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/blob/main/charts/qbittorrent-exporter/README.md#migrating-to-100) notes, which apply verbatim under this chart's top-level `qbittorrent-exporter:` key. **Metric names and labels differ** — re-import its Grafana dashboard after upgrading.
- **Hardened `securityContext` defaults (breaking).** All `exportarr` instances now run with `runAsNonRoot`, `runAsUser: 65532` (distroless nonroot), `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`, and `drop: [ALL]`. Fine for the stock `onedr0p/exportarr` image; override `exportarr.podSecurityContext`/`exportarr.securityContext` if you run a customized one.
- **Credentials render into a chart-managed Secret (behavior-preserving).** Inline `exportarr.apps.<app>[].apiKey` now populates a chart-managed per-instance `Secret`, referenced via `secretKeyRef`, instead of being set as a plaintext environment variable. No action needed — `existingSecret` still takes precedence over `apiKey` as before.

## Configuration Options
| Key | Type | Default | Description |
|-----|------|---------|-------------|
| exportarr.affinity | object | `{}` | affinity rules for exportarr pods |
| exportarr.apps | object | `{"bazarr":[{"apiKey":"","enabled":false,"existingSecret":{"name":"","secretKey":""},"extraEnv":{},"metrics":{},"name":"main","url":"","volumeMounts":[],"volumes":[]}],"lidarr":[{"apiKey":"","enabled":false,"existingSecret":{"name":"","secretKey":""},"extraEnv":{},"metrics":{},"name":"main","url":"","volumeMounts":[],"volumes":[]}],"prowlarr":[{"apiKey":"","enabled":false,"existingSecret":{"name":"","secretKey":""},"extraEnv":{},"metrics":{},"name":"main","url":"","volumeMounts":[],"volumes":[]}],"radarr":[{"apiKey":"","enabled":false,"existingSecret":{"name":"","secretKey":""},"extraEnv":{},"metrics":{},"name":"main","url":"","volumeMounts":[],"volumes":[]}],"readarr":[{"apiKey":"","enabled":false,"existingSecret":{"name":"","secretKey":""},"extraEnv":{},"metrics":{},"name":"main","url":"","volumeMounts":[],"volumes":[]}],"sabnzbd":[{"apiKey":"","enabled":false,"existingSecret":{"name":"","secretKey":""},"extraEnv":{},"metrics":{},"name":"main","url":"","volumeMounts":[],"volumes":[]}],"sonarr":[{"apiKey":"","enabled":false,"existingSecret":{"name":"","secretKey":""},"extraEnv":{},"metrics":{},"name":"main","url":"","volumeMounts":[],"volumes":[]}]}` | per-instance configuration; each key (radarr, sonarr, prowlarr, bazarr, lidarr, sabnzbd, readarr) takes a list of instance objects with fields: name, enabled, url, existingSecret.{name,secretKey}, apiKey, extraEnv, volumes, volumeMounts, metrics |
| exportarr.extraEnv | object | `{}` | extra environment variables applied to all exportarr instances |
| exportarr.fullnameOverride | string | `""` |  |
| exportarr.image.pullPolicy | string | `"IfNotPresent"` | image pull policy |
| exportarr.image.registry | string | `"ghcr.io"` | image registry |
| exportarr.image.repository | string | `"onedr0p/exportarr"` | image repository |
| exportarr.image.tag | string | `""` | overrides the image tag whose default is the chart appVersion |
| exportarr.imagePullSecrets | list | `[]` |  |
| exportarr.livenessProbe | object | `{"failureThreshold":5,"httpGet":{"path":"/healthz","port":"metrics"},"initialDelaySeconds":5,"periodSeconds":10,"successThreshold":1,"timeoutSeconds":3}` | liveness probe configuration for the exportarr instances |
| exportarr.metrics.enabled | bool | `true` | enable/disable prometheus podAnnotations and serviceMonitor |
| exportarr.metrics.podAnnotations | object | `{"prometheus.io/path":"/metrics","prometheus.io/port":"9707","prometheus.io/scrape":"true"}` | Add podAnnotations for prometheus scraping |
| exportarr.metrics.podAnnotations."prometheus.io/path" | string | `"/metrics"` | set the path for prometheus scraping |
| exportarr.metrics.podAnnotations."prometheus.io/port" | string | `"9707"` | set the port for prometheus scraping, should match the service port |
| exportarr.metrics.prometheusRule.enabled | bool | `false` | enable a PrometheusRule (requires the Prometheus Operator CRD) |
| exportarr.metrics.prometheusRule.labels | object | `{}` | additional labels for the PrometheusRule (e.g. to match your Prometheus ruleSelector) |
| exportarr.metrics.prometheusRule.rules | list | `[]` | alerting/recording rules rendered under a single group |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.additionalLabels | object | `{}` | set additional labels for serviceMonitor |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.enabled | bool | `false` | enable/disable serviceMonitor, if enabled podAnnotations will be ignored |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.interval | string | `"4m"` | how often to scrape for serviceMonitor |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.metricRelabelings | list | `[]` |  |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.path | string | `"/metrics"` | set the path for prometheus scraping for serviceMonitor |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.relabelings | list | `[]` |  |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.scrapeTimeout | string | `"60s"` | scrapeTimeout for serviceMonitor |
| exportarr.metrics.serviceMonitor.targetLabels | list | `[]` |  |
| exportarr.nameOverride | string | `""` |  |
| exportarr.nodeSelector | object | `{}` | node selector for exportarr pods |
| exportarr.podAnnotations | object | `{}` | annotations added to all exportarr pods |
| exportarr.podLabels | object | `{}` | labels added to all exportarr pods |
| exportarr.podSecurityContext | object | `{"fsGroup":65532,"runAsGroup":65532,"runAsNonRoot":true,"runAsUser":65532,"seccompProfile":{"type":"RuntimeDefault"}}` | pod-level security context applied to all exportarr instances |
| exportarr.readinessProbe | object | `{"failureThreshold":5,"httpGet":{"path":"/healthz","port":"metrics"},"initialDelaySeconds":5,"periodSeconds":10,"successThreshold":1,"timeoutSeconds":2}` | readiness probe configuration for the exportarr instances |
| exportarr.resources | object | `{}` | resource requests/limits for each exportarr deployment |
| exportarr.securityContext | object | `{"allowPrivilegeEscalation":false,"capabilities":{"drop":["ALL"]},"readOnlyRootFilesystem":true}` | container-level security context applied to all exportarr instances |
| exportarr.service.name | string | `"metrics"` | name for the service port |
| exportarr.service.port | int | `9707` | port for exportarr metrics scraping |
| exportarr.service.protocol | string | `"TCP"` | protocol for the service |
| exportarr.service.type | string | `"ClusterIP"` | Kubernetes Service type |
| exportarr.serviceAccount | object | `{"annotations":{},"create":false,"name":""}` | serviceAccount configuration |
| exportarr.startupProbe | object | `{"failureThreshold":5,"httpGet":{"path":"/healthz","port":"metrics"},"initialDelaySeconds":2,"periodSeconds":5,"successThreshold":1,"timeoutSeconds":2}` | startup probe configuration for the exportarr instances |
| exportarr.tests.enabled | bool | `true` | enable/disable the `helm test` connection-check Pod |
| exportarr.tests.image.pullPolicy | string | `"IfNotPresent"` | pull policy for the `helm test` connection-check image |
| exportarr.tests.image.registry | string | `"docker.io"` | registry for the `helm test` connection-check image |
| exportarr.tests.image.repository | string | `"busybox"` | repository for the `helm test` connection-check image |
| exportarr.tests.image.tag | string | `"1.36.1"` | tag for the `helm test` connection-check image |
| exportarr.tests.path | string | `"/healthz"` | path probed on each exportarr instance during the connection check |
| exportarr.tolerations | list | `[]` | tolerations for exportarr pods |
| exportarr.updateStrategy.type | string | `"Recreate"` | set the update strategy for the deployment |
| exportarr.volumeMounts | list | `[]` |  |
| exportarr.volumes | list | `[]` | additional volumes for all exportarr deployments |
| qbittorrent-exporter.enabled | bool | `false` | enable/disable the qbittorrent-exporter subchart |
| qbittorrent-exporter.extraEnv | object | `{}` | extra env applied to the qbittorrent-exporter subchart |
| qbittorrent-exporter.metrics.serviceMonitor.enabled | bool | `false` | enable/disable serviceMonitor for the qbittorrent-exporter subchart |
| qbittorrent-exporter.settings.auth.apiKey | string | `""` | qBittorrent API key. Maps to subchart `settings.auth.apiKey` |
| qbittorrent-exporter.settings.auth.existingSecret | string | `""` | use an existing secret instead of user/pass/apiKey. Maps to subchart `settings.auth.existingSecret` |
| qbittorrent-exporter.settings.auth.pass | string | `""` | qBittorrent WebUI password. Maps to subchart `settings.auth.pass` |
| qbittorrent-exporter.settings.auth.user | string | `""` | qBittorrent WebUI username. Maps to subchart `settings.auth.user` |
| qbittorrent-exporter.settings.config.baseUrl | string | `""` | qBittorrent base URL, e.g. https://qbit.example.org:443. Maps to subchart `settings.config.baseUrl` |
| qbittorrent-exporter.settings.config.insecureSkipVerify | bool | `false` | skip TLS certificate verification for the qBittorrent connection. Maps to subchart `settings.config.insecureSkipVerify` |
| tdarr-exporter.enabled | bool | `false` | enable/disable the tdarr-exporter subchart |
| tdarr-exporter.extraEnv | object | `{}` | extra env applied to the tdarr-exporter subchart |
| tdarr-exporter.metrics.serviceMonitor.enabled | bool | `false` | enable/disable serviceMonitor for the tdarr-exporter subchart |
| tdarr-exporter.settings.config.apiKey | string | `""` | Tdarr API key. Ignored if `existingSecret` is set. Maps to subchart `settings.config.apiKey` |
| tdarr-exporter.settings.config.existingSecret | object | `{"name":"","secretKey":""}` | use an existing secret instead of `apiKey`. Maps to subchart `settings.config.existingSecret` |
| tdarr-exporter.settings.config.log_level | string | `"info"` | exporter log level. Maps to subchart `settings.config.log_level` |
| tdarr-exporter.settings.config.url | string | `""` | Tdarr URL. If no protocol is given, defaults to https (e.g. `tdarr.example.com`). Maps to subchart `settings.config.url` |
| tdarr-exporter.settings.config.verify_ssl | bool | `true` | verify the Tdarr instance's SSL certificate. Maps to subchart `settings.config.verify_ssl` |

## Additional Exporters
This umbrella can optionally deploy two first-party exporters as subcharts. Configure each under its matching top-level key; every value in that subchart's own README applies verbatim under that key. The passthrough keys also appear in the [Configuration Options](#configuration-options) table above.

#### qbittorrent
Set `qbittorrent-exporter.enabled: true` to deploy it (wraps `martabal/qbittorrent-exporter`). Configure it under the `qbittorrent-exporter:` key — e.g. `qbittorrent-exporter.settings.config.baseUrl`. See the [qbittorrent-exporter chart README](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/qbittorrent-exporter) for the full value schema, and [Migrating to 3.0.0](#migrating-to-300) if upgrading from an earlier release.

#### Tdarr
Set `tdarr-exporter.enabled: true` to deploy it. Configure it under the `tdarr-exporter:` key — e.g. `tdarr-exporter.settings.config.url`. See the [tdarr-exporter chart README](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/tdarr-exporter) for the full value schema.

## Grafana Dashboards
Upstream [onedr0p/exportarr](https://github.com/onedr0p/exportarr/tree/main/examples/grafana) ships two sample dashboards. This chart bundles [`examples/dashboard.json`](https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/exportarr/examples/dashboard.json) — a fork of the upstream multi-instance dashboard extended to cover this chart's full `apps` list (adds `readarr` and `bazarr` instance variables and panels).

* https://github.com/onedr0p/exportarr
* https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/qbittorrent-exporter
* https://github.com/homeylab/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/tdarr-exporter
